repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
For Limited API>=3.15, we're expecting it'll support both freethreading and non-freethreading versions. Therefore, we'll need to add thread-safety guards in to work.

This does the changes that can be based around atomics - PyMutex and critical sections are not yet available so anything requiring those will have to wait.

comment_data.body:
There's probably a question here if we're better off with one define covering both, or two separate defines.

I think 2  are probably better for now, but it's a bit borderline
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -208,6 +210,8 @@
   #define CYTHON_COMPILING_IN_GRAAL 0
 
   #define CYTHON_COMPILING_IN_CPYTHON_FREETHREADING 0
+  // from 3.15+, the Limited API can also be used with freethreading
+  #define CYTHON_COMPILING_IN_LIMITED_API_FREETHREADING (__PYX_LIMITED_VERSION_HEX >= 0x030F0000)

comment_data.body: There was a lot of duplication in "import numpy" and "tstring" caching of objects so I generalized it.

commit.message:
Add thread safety guards in Limited API 3.15

For Limited API>=3.15, we're expecting it'll support both
freethreading and non-freethreading versions. Therefore, we'll need
to add thread-safety guards in to work.

This does the changes that can be based around atomics - PyMutex
and critical sections are not yet available so anything requiring
those will have to wait.

Add critical section handling

and move lazy object initialization into utility code since
it's pretty repetitive.
